# servicenow_flow (Data Source)

`servicenow_flow` data source can be used to retrieve information of a single Flow Designer flow in ServiceNow by name.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Look up an existing Flow Designer flow in ServiceNow by name.
data "servicenow_flow" "example" {
  name = "example-flow"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Display name of the Flow Designer flow.

### Read-Only

- `active` (Boolean) If set to 'true', the flow is active and can run.
- `application` (String) Sys ID of the sys_app application scope the flow belongs to (maps to sys_scope).
- `category` (String) Category of the flow: flow, subflow or action. Maps to ServiceNow's `type` column on sys_hub_flow.
- `description` (String) Description of the flow.
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `internal_name` (String) Auto-generated internal name derived from the display name.
- `status` (String) Publication status of the flow: draft or published.
- `trigger_type` (String) Trigger type for the flow, e.g. record_created, record_updated, record_deleted, scheduled. Note: triggers are normally stored on sys_hub_trigger_instance records associated with the flow; setting this here is informational only.
